Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.controller

Class MainGameController

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.controller.MainGameController
public class MainGameController extends Object
The single entry point that wires the whole game together. Owns the Game model and the BattlefieldView3D, and hands off input handling between the two phase-specific controllers -- ShipPlacementController first, then CombatController once the fleet is fully placed -- rather than doing either job itself. This keeps each controller focused on one HU and free to be tested or reused on its own.

Has two entry points, matching the two ways a match can begin: start() for a brand-new game (HU-1: fleet placement first), and resume() for a game reloaded from disk (HU-5: both fleets are already placed, so this skips placement entirely and jumps straight into combat).

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MainGameController(Game savedGame, BattlefieldView3D view, javafx.scene.Node pickSurface)
    Resumes an already-in-progress game loaded from disk (HU-5).
    MainGameController(String nickname, BattlefieldView3D view, javafx.scene.Node pickSurface)

    Description
    void
    addCombatListenerWhenReady(CombatListener listener)
    Convenience passthrough so callers only need to hold a reference to MainGameController, not to CombatController directly, to listen for turn changes and game-over -- returns silently (does nothing) if combat hasn't started yet.

    private void
    listenForKonamiCode()
    Registers the Konami Code (HU-3) as a scene-wide key listener, active for the rest of the game regardless of which controller currently owns mouse input.
    private Map<Ship,Ship3D>
    rebuildBoardView(Board board, javafx.scene.Group boardGroup, boolean hideIntactHulls)
    Builds the 3D shape for every ship on an already-placed board and adds it to the scene, then replays every shot already resolved on that board -- a miss becomes an X mark, a hit or sunk cell is applied to the matching ship via Ship3D.markHit(int).
    private void
    replayHit(Cell cell, Map<Ship,Ship3D> shipViews, Position position)
    Applies a single already-recorded hit/sunk cell to the 3D shape of whichever ship occupies it, via Ship3D.markHit(int).
    private void
    replayShotMarks(Board board, Map<Ship,Ship3D> shipViews, javafx.scene.Group boardGroup)
    Walks every cell of board and re-applies whatever shot outcome it already recorded (miss, hit, or sunk) to the matching 3D shapes.
    void
    resume()
    Jumps straight into combat with an already-placed, already in-progress game (HU-5).
    void
    start()
    Starts a new game: hands input over to fleet placement, and starts listening for the Konami Code (HU-3) for the whole rest of the game.
    private void
    startCombatPhase()
    Called once the human's fleet is fully placed: auto-places the machine's fleet (HU-4), mirrors it into CombatController, and hands input over to it.
    private void
    toggleCheatReveal()
    HU-3: toggles revealing every machine ship's hull at once (the "cheat" view), entered via KonamiCodeDetector.

  • Constructor Details

    • MainGameController

      public MainGameController(String nickname, BattlefieldView3D view, javafx.scene.Node pickSurface)
      Starts a brand-new game at HU-1.
      Parameters:
      nickname - the human player's chosen nickname
      view - the 3D battlefield to control
      pickSurface - the node mouse/keyboard input is read from (normally view itself)

    • MainGameController

      public MainGameController(Game savedGame, BattlefieldView3D view, javafx.scene.Node pickSurface)
      Resumes an already-in-progress game loaded from disk (HU-5).
      Parameters:
      savedGame - a game previously restored via SavedGameRepository.load()
      view - the 3D battlefield to control
      pickSurface - the node mouse/keyboard input is read from (normally view itself)

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts a new game: hands input over to fleet placement, and starts listening for the Konami Code (HU-3) for the whole rest of the game. Only valid on a controller created with the "new game" constructor.

    • resume

      public void resume()
      Jumps straight into combat with an already-placed, already in-progress game (HU-5). Only valid on a controller created with the "resume a saved game" constructor. If the save happened mid-way through the machine's turn, this also kicks the machine back into motion (see CombatController.resumeIfMachinesTurn()); otherwise the game would sit idle forever, with neither side able to act.

    • listenForKonamiCode

      private void listenForKonamiCode()
      Registers the Konami Code (HU-3) as a scene-wide key listener, active for the rest of the game regardless of which controller currently owns mouse input.

    • startCombatPhase

      private void startCombatPhase()
      Called once the human's fleet is fully placed: auto-places the machine's fleet (HU-4), mirrors it into CombatController, and hands input over to it.

    • rebuildBoardView

      private Map<Ship,Ship3D> rebuildBoardView(Board board, javafx.scene.Group boardGroup, boolean hideIntactHulls)
      Builds the 3D shape for every ship on an already-placed board and adds it to the scene, then replays every shot already resolved on that board -- a miss becomes an X mark, a hit or sunk cell is applied to the matching ship via Ship3D.markHit(int). Used for the machine's freshly-placed (and therefore shot-free) fleet in a new game, and for both fleets -- misses, hits and all -- when resuming a saved one.
      Parameters:
      board - an already-placed board to mirror into the scene
      boardGroup - the 3D group to add the ships to
      hideIntactHulls - true to hide each not-yet-sunk ship's hull (the machine's board, so the fleet stays secret -- see Ship3D.setHullVisible(boolean)), false to leave it visible (the player's own board)
      Returns:
      every ship on the board, mapped to its new 3D shape

    • replayShotMarks

      private void replayShotMarks(Board board, Map<Ship,Ship3D> shipViews, javafx.scene.Group boardGroup)
      Walks every cell of board and re-applies whatever shot outcome it already recorded (miss, hit, or sunk) to the matching 3D shapes.

    • replayHit

      private void replayHit(Cell cell, Map<Ship,Ship3D> shipViews, Position position)
      Applies a single already-recorded hit/sunk cell to the 3D shape of whichever ship occupies it, via Ship3D.markHit(int).

    • toggleCheatReveal

      private void toggleCheatReveal()
      HU-3: toggles revealing every machine ship's hull at once (the "cheat" view), entered via KonamiCodeDetector. Does nothing before the machine's fleet exists in the scene. Skips any ship that's already sunk: those stay permanently revealed and shouldn't be hidden again just because the cheat got toggled off.

    • getGame

      public Game getGame()
      Returns:
      the game being played

    • getPlacementController

      public ShipPlacementController getPlacementController()
      Returns:
      the placement controller, or null if this game was resumed from a save (no placement phase)

    • getCombatController

      public CombatController getCombatController()
      Returns:
      the combat controller, or null before combat has actually started

    • addCombatListenerWhenReady

      public void addCombatListenerWhenReady(CombatListener listener)
      Convenience passthrough so callers only need to hold a reference to MainGameController, not to CombatController directly, to listen for turn changes and game-over -- returns silently (does nothing) if combat hasn't started yet. Only valid for a "new game" controller; for a resumed game, getCombatController() is already non-null immediately after resume() returns, so the listener can be attached directly instead.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to attach once combat starts
